chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Cr. Bail Appl. No. S- 166 & 274 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 16.03.2026 For orders on office objections For hearing of main case Mr. Aijaz Ali Bhutto, Advocate for applicant in Cr. Bail Appl. No. S- 274 of 2026 Mr. Mumtaz Ali Advocate holds brief for Mr. Zahid Hussain Khoso, Advocate for applicant in Cr. Bail Appl. No. S- 166 of 2026 Ms. Sana Memon, APG The offences with which the applicant(s) are charged are either bailable or fall within the non-prohibitory clause of Section 497 Cr.P.C. Keeping in mind the principles enunciated in the case of Tariq Bashir & 5 others v. The State (PLD 1995 S.C. 34), I do not see any exceptional or extra-ordinary reason to deny bail to the applicant; secondly there is an inordinate delay of two months in registration of this FIR for which no explanation is on record; mala fide can therefore not be conclusively ruled out at this preliminary stage. Given the above, the applicant(s) are admitted to post-arrest bail against a surety of Rs. 1,00,000/- (One Lac) each and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of learned trial court.. JUDGE karar_hussain/PS*
